danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile entities within cave environments
danger_signals.meaning: Threats that reduce player hit points; must be avoided or destroyed with rocket gun

danger_signals.visual_form: Physical barriers and hazards activated by switches
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental dangers that block progress or cause damage; can be deactivated via puzzle solving

danger_signals.visual_form: Hit point counter at three maximum (visual representation not specified in source)
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability state; losing all hit points returns player to level start

safety_signals.meaning: Supercharges rocket gun for one-shot enemy elimination; temporary offensive advantage
Reverses gravity; allows ceiling traversal for evasion and puzzle solving

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible item visualized as stop sign symbols
safety_signals.meaning: Freezes all on-screen enemies; enables safe passage through danger zones
Replenishes rocket gun capacity; player begins with five rockets
Finishing level with full hit points grants bonus; signals safe completion state

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Crystal collection objective; numbered caves (1-16) in hub level

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple distinct enemy types implied by gameplay mechanics (some frozen by Stop Signs, all targetable by rockets)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed throughout cave interiors; indicate cave danger level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cave entrance visible in main hub level; entrance door representation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mylo can enter; level state is fresh with all enemies and obstacles active

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Puzzle elements: colored doors corresponding to levers; active switches and deactivable obstacles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol actively; obstacles block paths until switches trigger state changes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level exit reached; bonus score tally screen ($50,000 bonus display)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player returns to hub; cave remains in 'completed' state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level exit triggered without crystal collection; return to hub
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All progress in that cave negated; must restart if re-entered

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hit point counter reaches zero; death animation/screen transition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level restart; player reset to cave entrance with full health

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual inversion of level geometry; Mylo sprite inverted/repositioned on ceiling
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player walks on ceiling; gravity-dependent obstacles reverse behavior; temporary mechanic overlay

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy sprites shift to frozen/stasis visual state (pause effect)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All enemies cease movement; player can freely navigate through normally dangerous zones

environmental_storytelling.example: 16 distinct caves in hub level with player freedom to choose order; visual design implies increasing difficulty/complexity as player advances
Colored doors matched to lever switches; visual consistency between lock color and switch output tells player puzzle dependency structure
Crystal collection as currency proxy; score display in dollars ($50,000 bonus) reinforces protagonist's get-rich-quick motivations across three volume plot
Main level layout presenting all 16 cave entrances simultaneously; visual scale and arrangement communicate scope of adventure
Distinct collectible labels (P-Pill, G-Pill, Stop Signs) function as visual language teaching players mechanic availability without text

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Side-scrolling platformer locomotion; constrained by gravity and obstacles
Can fire projectiles to destroy enemies and breakable obstacles; ammunition limited (starts with 5 rockets)
Gravity inverts; Mylo can walk on ceiling; enables alternative pathfinding and evasion
Rocket gun supercharged; single shot eliminates all enemy types; temporary offensive advantage
Patrol cave areas; approach and attack player; vulnerable to rocket fire
Freeze in place; cease all movement and aggression; become harmless obstacles
Block pathways; remain in default state until lever pulled
State change triggered by matching lever; colored door opens; path clears or new hazard appears

notes: Crystal Caves employs a minimal visual language typical of 1991 MS-DOS platformers. The game communicates world-state primarily through: (1) spatial level design (hub model with 16 cave entrances), (2) visual state flags (locked/unlocked doors, enemy presence, collectible pickups), and (3) player avatar transformation (inverted sprite during G-Pill). Danger is signaled through enemy presence and active obstacles; safety through power-up availability and puzzle solutions. The three-episode narrative structure maps to distinct world-states in the plot, but these are communicated textually rather than visually within gameplay. No faction conflict or political situation is present; the game is a resource-collection adventure. Environmental storytelling relies on puzzle-lock visual consistency and economic metaphor (crystals as currency).
